User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account in the Narros platform, used for authentication and identifying workspace owners/members.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

name.description: The user's full name as registered or obtained from the authentication provider.

email.description: The user's email address, used for identification and communication.

plan.description: The user's subscription plan, determining access to features and quotas.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

Workspace.title: Workspace
Workspace.description: Defines a workspace with its name, type, sector, owner, and explicit member list.

sector.description: The business sector of the workspace.

ownerId.description: The UID of the user who owns the workspace.

members.description: A list of UIDs of users who are members of the workspace.

knowledgeStatus.description: The current status of the workspace's knowledge base.

publishedAt.description: Timestamp of when the knowledge base was first published.

lastSyncAt.description: Timestamp of the last knowledge base synchronization.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the workspace was created.

Analysis.title: Analysis
Analysis.description: Stores details of content analyses performed.

Knowledge.title: Knowledge
Knowledge.description: Represents a structured knowledge category and its items.

Training.title: Training
Training.description: Stores individual training modules generated from an analysis.

Playbook.title: Playbook
Playbook.description: Stores operational playbooks generated from an analysis.

Insight.title: Insight
Insight.description: Stores identified gaps, opportunities, or risks from an analysis.

ChatMessage.title: Chat Message
ChatMessage.description: Stores individual chat messages for the AI assistant.

definition.description: Stores user profile data. Authenticated users (`request.auth.uid`) can only read/write their own document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Defines a workspace with its name, type, sector, owner, and explicit member list. Includes `ownerId` and `members` map for direct authorization on this document and subsequent denormalization into related entities.

params.name: workspaceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the workspace.

definition.description: Stores details of content analyses performed. Includes denormalized `workspaceId`, `ownerId`, and `members` map for authorization independence and QAPs.

params.name: analysisId
params.description: The unique identifier of the analysis document.

definition.description: Represents a structured knowledge category and its items generated from an analysis. Includes denormalized `workspaceId`, `ownerId`, and `members` map for authorization independence and QAPs.

params.name: knowledgeId
params.description: The unique identifier for a knowledge category document.

definition.description: Stores individual training modules generated from an analysis. Includes denormalized `workspaceId`, `ownerId`, and `members` map for authorization independence and QAPs.

params.name: moduleId
params.description: The unique identifier for a training module document.

definition.description: Stores operational playbooks generated from an analysis. Includes denormalized `workspaceId`, `ownerId`, and `members` map for authorization independence and QAPs.

params.name: playbookId
params.description: The unique identifier for a playbook document.

definition.description: Stores identified gaps, opportunities, or risks from an analysis. Includes denormalized `workspaceId`, `ownerId`, and `members` map for authorization independence and QAPs.

params.name: insightId
params.description: The unique identifier for an insight document.

definition.description: Stores individual chat messages for the AI assistant. Includes denormalized `workspaceId`, `ownerId`, and `members` map for authorization independence, and `userId` of the sender for message ownership.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for a chat message document.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and Query-Agnostic Permissions (QAPs) by refactoring subcollections into top-level collections and denormalizing authorization context into every document. This approach directly addresses the critical need to avoid hierarchical authorization dependencies and complex `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules.

Specifically, each document within the `analyses`, `knowledge`, `training`, `playbooks`, `insights`, and `chatHistory` collections will now explicitly contain the `workspaceId` it belongs to, its `ownerId`, and a `members` map (`{[uid: string]: "role"}`).

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** By denormalizing the `ownerId` and `members` map into every document, security rules can directly evaluate access permissions based on the `resource.data` of the document being accessed. For example, a rule for `/analyses/{analysisId}` would check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId || request.auth.uid in resource.data.members`. This completely eliminates the need for `get(/workspaces/$(workspaceId))` calls, which are non-atomic, expensive, and difficult to debug. This also enables atomic creation/update of documents without needing to read parent permissions first, fulfilling Mandate A.

**QAPs (Query-Agnostic Permissions):** Moving these collections to the top level (e.g., `/analyses` instead of `/workspaces/{workspaceId}/analyses`) and including the `workspaceId` as a field, allows for powerful and secure `list` queries. Clients can now query `/analyses` with `where('workspaceId', '==', someWorkspaceId).where('members.' + request.auth.uid, 'exists', true)`. The rules can then directly validate `resource.data.members[request.auth.uid] == 'owner' || resource.data.members[request.auth.uid] == 'member'`, ensuring that the client only receives documents it is authorized to access. This approach adheres to Mandate B (Structural Segregation) by ensuring that all documents within a given collection share the same authorization fields and, therefore, a consistent security posture, simplifying rule logic and improving query efficiency.
